meta.title: aish — Your AI Copilot for the Command Line
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI copilot for your terminal. It wraps the commands developers use every day—commit, pr, review, changelog, ask, and fix—into built-in subcommands that generate clean summaries and troubleshoot output. Free, MIT-licensed software.

hero.badge: v0.12 shipped — set `commit.instructions` in your config to shape commit-message style (gitmoji, longer subjects, a mandatory body)

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI copilot for your terminal.
turns your staged diff into a clean commit message you can accept, edit, or reject. Reach for
to open the generated message in your $EDITOR before it lands—no setup beyond the binary itself. There's also
to turn plain language into a shell command, behind a confirm/edit gate.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

items.title: Seamless Git workflows
items.body: Let aish handle the heavy lifting. aish commit crafts Conventional Commits from your staged diff. aish pr drafts PR titles and descriptions, aish review flags issues by severity, and aish changelog generates release notes. Identical requests hit a local cache for speed, every generation is logged for transparency, and aish usage gives you clear token and cost reports. When a build or test breaks, aish fix runs the command and diagnoses the failure on the spot.

items.title: Truly provider-agnostic
items.body: Connect Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Groq, Ollama, or Kilo through a single, unified config. Never get locked into a single vendor—swap models instantly with a simple flag.

items.title: Open source & fully transparent
items.body: Free and MIT-licensed, distributed as a single, static Rust binary. Read the source, audit the design, or submit a patch—it’s been built in the open from day one.
